compiled_state.title: Assert one shared concurrency-rejection identity across CLI/lib/MCP
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What
The cross-interface concurrency contract is asserted weakly:
- tests/interfaces/adapters.py (L62-67,154-155,247-257): every transition/claim adapter collapses ALL failures to `return False` (MCP via except Exception; CLI via returncode==0 only) — so parity rejection tests cannot tell exit-10 from any other failure.
- tests/interfaces/facades/test_mcp.py:366-388: MCP concurrency identity asserted via prose substrings ("transition rejected"/"no longer") — implementation-pinned, not a typed code.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parity adapters surface a STRUCTURED rejection outcome (not bare bool)
- [ ] Assert CLI rejection is specifically returncode==10, lib raises ConcurrencyError, MCP raises a tool error — and that the three carry ONE shared structured identity (code/class), not prose wording
- [ ] Tests fail if an interface rejects for the wrong reason (e.g. not-found vs concurrency)

comments.body: Done. adapters.py now returns a structured Outcome{ok,code,error_type} (frozen dataclass, truthy iff ok) with is_concurrency keyed on CONCURRENCY_CODE=10. Library reads exc.returncode/type; CLI reads exit code (10->ConcurrencyError); MCP unwraps ToolError.__cause__ (the chained ConcurrencyError). Parity rejection tests now assert is_concurrency + code==10 + error_type=='ConcurrencyError', failing on wrong-reason rejects. test_mcp.py asserts the typed chained ConcurrencyError(returncode 10) not prose; added a not-found discrimination test. Temp-break verified red. Full interfaces suite green (116 across 2 orders).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
